input.tenant_message: Hi, just wanted to let you know the toilet in the master bathroom has been running nonstop for about a week now. It makes that hissing/trickling sound all the time even when no one has flushed it. I jiggled the handle and it stops for a bit but then starts right back up. It's not a huge deal but I figured I should report it since it's probably wasting water.

ai_output.recommended_action: Schedule a non-urgent plumbing visit to replace the flapper valve or fill valve inside the toilet tank — a continuously running toilet typically indicates a worn flapper or faulty float mechanism, both inexpensive repairs. This should be addressed within 1-2 weeks to avoid unnecessary water waste and higher utility bills.
